steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states a general principle (sleek vehicles are more aerodynamic), then illustrates it with a contrast: the Cessna 310 (sleek) versus a semitruck (bulky). The blank must introduce that Cessna example as a concrete illustration of the principle.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — signals contradiction or contrast: The Cessna is not contrasting the general claim — it is supporting it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For example': Introduces the streamlined nose as a specific instance of the principle about sleek vehicles. Matches the passage's pattern exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — signals simultaneous action or addition: The sentence is not listing parallel events; it is exemplifying. ✗
  • Choice D — signals listing or sequence: No sequence is implied; the Cessna is an example, not a next step. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
